curriculum.subject: Physics
curriculum.overallDescription: 5 units, 6 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand the need for measurement and systems of units including fundamental and derived units.
Apply international standards for SI units, symbols, and nomenclature of physical quantities.
Determine significant figures and calculate uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis and apply it to solve problems.
Define frame of reference and describe motion in a straight line.
Apply elementary concepts of differentiation and integration to describe motion.
Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity.
Analyze uniformly accelerated motion using graphical methods (v-t, x-t) and calculus-based relations.
Differentiate between scalar and vector quantities and perform vector addition, subtraction, and resolution.
Calculate scalar and vector products of vectors.
Analyze cases of uniform velocity and acceleration in a plane including projectile motion.

units.description: Motion in a Straight Line, Motion in a Plane
Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body

suspicious_chapter_names: Practical Examination for Visually Impaired Students Class XI

competency_groups.title: Units and Measurements
competency_groups.description: Emphasis on basic conceptual understanding of measurement, SI units, symbols, nomenclature, significant figures, dimensional analysis, and uncertainty determination.
competencies: Understand the need for measurement and systems of units including fundamental and derived units.
Apply international standards for SI units, symbols, and nomenclature of physical quantities.
Determine significant figures and calculate uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis and apply it to solve problems.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit I: Physical World and Measurements

competency_groups.title: Motion in a Straight Line
competency_groups.description: Develop conceptual competence in describing motion using calculus, graphs, and equations for uniform and non-uniform acceleration.
competencies: Define frame of reference and describe motion in a straight line.
Apply elementary concepts of differentiation and integration to describe motion.
Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity.
Analyze uniformly accelerated motion using graphical methods (v-t, x-t) and calculus-based relations.

competency_groups.title: Motion in a Plane
competency_groups.description: Master vector operations and apply them to analyze projectile motion and uniform circular motion.
competencies: Differentiate between scalar and vector quantities and perform vector addition, subtraction, and resolution.
Calculate scalar and vector products of vectors.
Analyze cases of uniform velocity and acceleration in a plane including projectile motion.
Describe uniform circular motion.

competency_groups.title: Laws of Motion
competency_groups.description: Develop problem-solving abilities regarding forces, momentum conservation, friction, and circular motion dynamics.
competencies: Explain intuitive concepts of force, inertia, Newton's laws (1st, 2nd, 3rd), impulse, and linear momentum conservation.
Analyze equilibrium of concurrent forces including static, kinetic, rolling friction, and lubrication.
Apply dynamics principles to uniform circular motion, centripetal force, banked roads, and level curves.

competency_groups.title: Work, Energy and Power
competency_groups.description: Apply work-energy theorems to analyze collisions, potential energy variations, and power in various physical scenarios.
competencies: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces.
Apply the work-energy theorem involving kinetic energy and power.
Analyze conservative vs. non-conservative forces, potential energy (including springs), and motion in vertical circles.
Evaluate elastic and inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit IV: Work, Energy and Power

competency_groups.title: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
competency_groups.description: Understand rotational dynamics, center of mass concepts, torque, angular momentum conservation, and moment of inertia.
competencies: Determine the center of mass for particle systems and rigid bodies (e.g., uniform rod).
Apply laws of motion to rotation including torque, angular momentum, and their conservation.
Analyze equilibrium conditions for rigid bodies in rotational motion.
Calculate moment of inertia and radius of gyration for simple geometrical objects.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit V: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
competency_groups.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

competency_groups.title: Gravitation
competency_groups.description: Apply universal laws of gravitation to planetary motion, satellite dynamics, and gravitational potential.
competencies: State Kepler's laws and the Universal Law of Gravitation.
Analyze acceleration due to gravity variations with altitude and depth.
Calculate gravitational potential energy, escape speed, orbital velocity, and total energy of orbiting satellites.

competency_groups.title: Mechanical Properties of Solids
competency_groups.description: Understand elastic behavior, stress-strain relationships, and material properties like Young's modulus.
competencies: Explain elasticity, Hooke's law, and the relationship between stress and strain.
Define and apply concepts of Young's modulus, bulk modulus, shear modulus (qualitative), Poisson's ratio, and elastic energy.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit VII: Properties of Bulk Matter

competency_groups.title: Mechanical Properties of Fluids
competency_groups.description: Apply principles of fluid statics and dynamics including pressure, viscosity, surface tension, and Bernoulli's theorem.
competencies: Analyze Pascal's law applications (hydraulic lift/brakes) and fluid column pressure.
Explain viscosity, Stokes' law, terminal velocity, streamline/turbulent flow, and critical velocity.
Apply Bernoulli's theorem to Torricelli's law and dynamic lift.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit VII: Properties of Bulk Matter

competency_groups.title: Thermal Properties of Matter
competency_groups.description: Understand heat transfer mechanisms, thermal expansion, specific heats, and blackbody radiation laws.
competencies: Distinguish between heat and temperature; explain anomalous expansion of water.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit VII: Properties of Bulk Matter

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics
competency_groups.description: Apply laws of thermodynamics to analyze state variables, processes (isothermal, adiabatic), and energy transformations.
competencies: Define thermal equilibrium, temperature, zeroth law, heat, work, and internal energy.

competency_groups.title: Kinetic Theory of Gases
competency_groups.description: Connect macroscopic gas properties to microscopic molecular behavior using kinetic theory assumptions.
competencies: Derive and apply the equation of state for a perfect gas.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit IX: Behavior of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

competency_groups.title: Oscillations
competency_groups.description: Analyze periodic motion, simple harmonic motion (SHM), energy in oscillation, and the physics of pendulums.
competencies: Describe periodic functions, time period, frequency, and displacement as a function of time.

competency_groups.title: Waves
competency_groups.description: Understand wave motion types, superposition principles, standing waves, beats, and interference phenomena.
competencies: Distinguish between transverse and longitudinal waves.

internal_assessment: The record, to be submitted by the students, at the time of their annual examination, has to include: Record of at least 8 Experiments (with 4 from each section), to be performed by the students. Record of at least 6 Activities (with 3 each from section A and section B), to be performed by the students. Report of the project carried out by the students.

practical_assessment.component: Two experiments one from each section

project_assessment.description: Report of the project carried out by the students.

project_assessment.component: Viva on experiments, activities and project

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit I: Physical World and Measurements
outcomes: Understand the need for measurement and systems of units including fundamental and derived units.
Apply international standards for SI units, symbols, and nomenclature of physical quantities.
Determine significant figures and calculate uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis and apply it to solve problems.
Define frame of reference and describe motion in a straight line.
Apply elementary concepts of differentiation and integration to describe motion.
Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity.
Analyze uniformly accelerated motion using graphical methods (v-t, x-t) and calculus-based relations.
Differentiate between scalar and vector quantities and perform vector addition, subtraction, and resolution.
Calculate scalar and vector products of vectors.
Analyze cases of uniform velocity and acceleration in a plane including projectile motion.
Explain intuitive concepts of force, inertia, Newton's laws (1st, 2nd, 3rd), impulse, and linear momentum conservation.
Analyze equilibrium of concurrent forces including static, kinetic, rolling friction, and lubrication.
Apply dynamics principles to uniform circular motion, centripetal force, banked roads, and level curves.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit IV: Work, Energy and Power
outcomes: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces.
Apply the work-energy theorem involving kinetic energy and power.
Analyze conservative vs. non-conservative forces, potential energy (including springs), and motion in vertical circles.
Evaluate elastic and inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions.

lesson_planning_intelligence.unit_name: Unit I: Physical World and Measurements
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Vernier Callipers, Screw Gauge, Spherometer experiments required.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Significant figures and dimensional analysis problems.
Vector operations and relative velocity problems.
Free body diagrams and Newton's laws applications.

projects.title: Investigatory Project
projects.details: Report of the project carried out by the students.

internal_assessment: The record, to be submitted by the students, at the time of their annual examination, has to include: Record of at least 8 Experiments (with 4 from each section), to be performed by the students. Record of at least 6 Activities (with 3 each from section A and section B), to be performed by the students. Report of the project carried out by the students.

practical_assessment.component: Two experiments one from each section

project_assessment.description: Report of the project carried out by the students.

project_assessment.component: Viva on experiments, activities and project
